scenario_title: The Zig-Zag Van vs. The Straight Truck
scenario_description: A white delivery van navigates a construction zone with shifting lanes, followed later by a similar-looking white box truck entering the same area. The user tracks the original van to see if it emerges in the clear lane after the cones.

turns.text: Check out this white van entering the construction zone. It's weaving between the orange cones on the left side of the road.
turns.image_description: A daytime street scene showing a white delivery van (Ford Transit style) entering a construction zone marked by bright orange traffic cones. The van is positioned in the left-most available lane, angled sharply to the right as it begins to zig-zag around a cone. The background shows a blurred city skyline. The van has a distinct blue stripe along its lower bumper.

turns.text: Got it. That white van with the blue stripe is definitely taking a sharp path through the cones. Looks like the lane shifts are forcing it to move left and right quickly.
Here's the next shot. It's still navigating the maze of cones.
turns.image_description: The same white delivery van from Turn 1 is now further into the construction zone. It is captured mid-turn, angling back to the left to avoid another row of orange cones. The blue stripe on the bumper is clearly visible. The lighting is slightly brighter than the first image, suggesting the sun is moving or the angle changed. No other vehicles are in the immediate vicinity.

turns.text: It's holding its own pretty well despite the tight squeeze. The zig-zag pattern is intense there.
Wait, look at this one. A different vehicle just entered the same spot where the first one started.
turns.image_description: A wide shot of the entrance to the construction zone. A large white box truck (Isuzu NPR style, taller and boxier than the van) is entering the zone. It does not have a blue stripe; instead, it has red lettering on the side that reads 'LOGISTICS'. It is driving straight towards the first set of orange cones, unlike the previous vehicle which was already angled.

turns.text: That looks like a bigger rig compared to the first one. Different shape, and I don't see that blue stripe on the bumper.
Okay, here is the exit point. The construction zone ends and the road clears up. Which vehicle made it through?
turns.image_description: A view of the road immediately after the construction zone ends. The orange cones stop abruptly on the left. In the clear lane, a white vehicle is driving away from the camera. This vehicle is the original Ford Transit-style van with the distinctive blue stripe on the lower bumper. The white box truck with red lettering is NOT visible in this frame; it appears to be either behind the camera or stuck further back in the zone.

turns.text: I see a white vehicle clearing the zone. It matches the profile of the first car we saw.

Based on what we've seen so far, did the specific van that was zig-zagging earlier make it to the clear lane, or was it replaced by the new truck? A) The zig-zagging van made it through. B) The new logistics truck replaced it. C) Neither vehicle is visible.

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. Turn 1 & 3 establish the target entity: a white Ford Transit-style van with a blue stripe on the bumper, performing a zig-zag maneuver. 2. Turn 5 introduces a distractor entity: a larger white box truck with red 'LOGISTICS' lettering and no blue stripe. 3. Turn 7 shows the exit. The vehicle present is the Ford Transit-style van with the blue stripe. 4. By cross-referencing the visual features (blue stripe, vehicle shape) from Turns 1/3 with Turn 7, we confirm the original van is the one that exited. The distractor truck (Turn 5) is absent in Turn 7.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must distinguish between two visually similar white vehicles based on specific details (blue stripe vs. red lettering, van shape vs. box truck shape) established in separate turns and track the correct entity across time, ignoring the distractor introduced in the middle turn.

_meta.scenario: A white delivery van enters a construction zone where lanes shift left, causing the van to zig-zag between two rows of orange cones while maintaining speed. The conversation requires identifying if this specific van is still present in the clear lane after the construction zone ends, distinguishing it from a similar-looking truck that entered later.
